jokes.text: Core meltdown in 10 seconds... Meltdown averted. Good boy!
jokes.setup: Nuclear plant alarm counting down to disaster
jokes.punchline: Homer's treated like a pet dog after accidentally fixing it
jokes.explanation: Sets up Homer's incompetence theme perfectly - he succeeds through dumb luck and gets condescending praise. Good economy and character work.

jokes.text: We make cookies here. Mr. Burns's Old-Fashioned Good-Time Extra-Chewy--
jokes.setup: Nuclear regulators arrive for surprise inspection
jokes.punchline: Burns frantically improvises that they make cookies, complete with fake product name
jokes.explanation: Classic Burns - his evil schemes mixed with corporate speak. The specificity of the fake cookie brand name elevates it.

jokes.text: The watchdog of public safety. Is there any lower form of life?
jokes.setup: Nuclear regulators are supposed to protect public safety
jokes.punchline: Burns sees safety regulators as the lowest form of life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - his disdain for anyone interfering with his profit. Clean line with good economy.

jokes.text: Duh, Homer, why are we down here? Oh, geez! I told ya, Bernie-- to guard the bee!
jokes.setup: Incompetent workers hidden in basement during inspection
jokes.punchline: Homer's important job is guarding a single bee, which he takes seriously
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdism - the idea of 'guarding the bee' as a serious job at a nuclear plant. Homer's earnestness sells it.

jokes.text: No wonder Smithers made me head bee guy.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining his bee-guarding superiority
jokes.punchline: There's apparently a hierarchy of bee-related positions
jokes.explanation: Extends the bee absurdity - Homer's pride in his meaningless promotion is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: No, I'm afraid he couldn't 'bee' here.
jokes.setup: Regulators asking for Homer Simpson
jokes.punchline: Burns makes a bee pun while lying about Homer's whereabouts
jokes.explanation: Standard pun humor, elevated slightly by the callback to the bee situation. Burns chuckling at his own joke is in character.

jokes.text: Oh, yes, he's in Geneva... chairing a conference on nuclear fission.
jokes.setup: Need to explain Homer's absence to regulators
jokes.punchline: Burns claims incompetent Homer is at a prestigious nuclear conference
jokes.explanation: Good ironic contrast between Homer's actual incompetence and the prestigious lie. Burns's confidence in the absurd story works.

jokes.text: D'oh! The bee bit my bottom! Now my bottom's big!
jokes.setup: Homer was supposed to guard the bee safely
jokes.punchline: He gets stung and his alliterative complaint about swelling
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy payoff to the bee setup. Homer's childish complaint and alliteration is endearing but not exceptional.

jokes.text: What the hell are you talking about?
jokes.setup: Regulators explain technical nuclear simulation procedure
jokes.punchline: Homer's blunt admission that he doesn't understand basic job requirements
jokes.explanation: Homer's honest confusion is funny and perfectly in character. Simple but effective character moment.

jokes.text: Just poke blindly at the controls until they let you go.
jokes.setup: Homer asked what to do during nuclear simulation
jokes.punchline: His strategy is random button-pushing until he's allowed to leave
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about nuclear safety combined with Homer's lazy approach to work. Quotable and captures his character perfectly.

jokes.text: Oh! This can't be happening! [simulation causes real meltdown]
jokes.setup: Told it's just a harmless simulator
jokes.punchline: Homer somehow causes actual nuclear disaster during fake test
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - Homer's incompetence transcends the safety measures. The impossibility makes it funnier.

jokes.text: For the love of God, sir! There are two seats! I like to put my feet up.
jokes.setup: Emergency evacuation during meltdown
jokes.punchline: Burns refuses to let Smithers in helicopter because he wants legroom
jokes.explanation: Perfect Burns characterization - his selfishness even in life-threatening situations. The casual cruelty and Smithers's desperation create great contrast.

jokes.text: Must destroy mankind. Ooh! Lunchtime.
jokes.setup: Mutant creature emerges from nuclear accident
jokes.punchline: Monster's world-ending mission interrupted by mundane lunch break
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection joke. The mundane interrupting the apocalyptic is perfectly executed with great economy.

jokes.text: There wasn't any nuclear material in the truck!
jokes.setup: Trying to figure out how Homer caused meltdown
jokes.punchline: Homer's incompetence defies physical laws - no nuclear material needed
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of Homer's impossible incompetence. The scientific impossibility makes it funnier.

jokes.text: The box. The box!
jokes.setup: Burns offering bribe with washer/dryer vs mystery box choice
jokes.punchline: Federal regulator gets excited about game show format during corruption
jokes.explanation: Funny juxtaposition of game show excitement with serious corruption. Good but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Burns offering washer/dryer or mystery box, Homer chooses 'The box. The box!'
jokes.setup: Burns offers bribe in game show format
jokes.punchline: Homer's childlike excitement for mystery box over practical appliances
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Homer's impulsiveness and childlike nature. The game show parody is competent but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Gentlemen, I've decided there will be no investigation. Now, if you'll excuse me, I'll go away.
jokes.setup: Regulator was just insisting on investigation
jokes.punchline: Immediately reverses position and awkwardly exits after bribe
jokes.explanation: The awkward phrasing of 'I'll go away' adds to the corruption humor, but fairly standard corruption satire.

jokes.text: Well, I have the feeling you'll be... dropping the charges. The painters moved your desk, sir.
jokes.setup: Burns dramatically threatens regulators while walking
jokes.punchline: His threatening gesture fails because he's at the wrong spot
jokes.explanation: Classic visual gag undermining Burns's dramatic moment. The timing and Smithers's matter-of-fact explanation work well.

jokes.text: Would you like to come in for tea and marshmallow squares? Yes, he would.
jokes.setup: Burns arrives with lawyers to pressure Homer
jokes.punchline: Marge innocently offers wholesome snacks, Burns accepts for whole intimidating entourage
jokes.explanation: Nice contrast between Marge's innocence and Burns' intimidation tactics. His immediate acceptance is funny.

jokes.text: College? Ha! Barber or clown?
jokes.setup: Homer told he needs to go to college
jokes.punchline: Bart assumes those are the only college options for Homer
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Bart's quick wit and assessment of his father's capabilities. Economical and in character.

jokes.text: You must find the jade monkey... before the next full moon. Actually, sir, we found the jade monkey.
jokes.setup: Burns giving Homer mysterious quest-like instructions
jokes.punchline: Smithers reveals it was just lost in Burns's car all along
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor - Burns's dramatic quest undermined by mundane reality. The jade monkey line became iconic.

jokes.text: That dog has somebody's ham! This I gotta see!
jokes.setup: Homer about to sign important college application
jokes.punchline: Gets completely distracted by random dog situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer moment - his curiosity about absurd situations overriding important tasks. Very in character.

jokes.text: Is TV Guide a book? Son of Sniglet? Katherine Hepburn's Me?
jokes.setup: Application asks for three favorite books
jokes.punchline: Homer's pitiful attempts to name books show his limited reading
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Homer's cultural limitations. The specific wrong choices feel authentic.

jokes.text: It was the most I ever threw up, and it changed my life forever.
jokes.setup: Homer writing college application essay
jokes.punchline: Treats vomiting as profound life-changing experience
jokes.explanation: Great Homer logic - finding profound meaning in gross bodily functions. The earnest delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: He looks crazy. Agreed. Reading his essay would only waste valuable seconds.
jokes.setup: Admissions officers reviewing Homer's application photo
jokes.punchline: Photo is so bad they won't even read his application
jokes.explanation: Visual gag callback to Homer's terrible photo. The bureaucratic dismissiveness is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Dean Bitterman, I hope nothing unsavory happens during my visit. As you know, I am the president of the United States.
jokes.setup: College movie parody with student visiting campus
jokes.punchline: Student casually mentions being the President
jokes.explanation: Movie parody absurdism - the casual delivery of impossible information. Standard parody humor.

jokes.text: Your bra bomb better work, Nerdlinger.
jokes.setup: College prank movie parody
jokes.punchline: Ridiculous weapon name in casual threat
jokes.explanation: Generic college movie parody humor. The absurd weapon name has some appeal but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Secretary of partying down!
jokes.setup: President impressed by student's pranks
jokes.punchline: Offers fake cabinet position based on partying
jokes.explanation: Standard political satire mixing official language with slang. Not particularly clever or memorable.

jokes.text: D'oh! D'oh! D'oh! Whoo-hoo! A flyer for a hardware store!
jokes.setup: Homer opening college rejection letters
jokes.punchline: Gets excited about junk mail after multiple rejections
jokes.explanation: Good escalation pattern with the D'oh's followed by misplaced enthusiasm. Homer's optimism despite failure is endearing.

jokes.text: [Burns intimidating admissions board while growling menacingly]
jokes.setup: Admissions board objects to Homer's admission
jokes.punchline: Burns literally growls like an animal to intimidate them
jokes.explanation: Burns's animalistic behavior contrasts with his usual sophistication. Visual gag works well with his character.

jokes.text: I'm giving you the beating of your life! Look, if-- Stop that. If you wanted him that badly, why didn't you just say so?
jokes.setup: Burns attacking admissions officer for rejecting Homer
jokes.punchline: Officer casually negotiates while being beaten
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy with the absurdity of calm negotiation during violence. The bureaucratic resignation is funny.

jokes.text: I won't need my high school diploma anymore!
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating college acceptance
jokes.punchline: Thinks college replaces rather than builds on high school education
jokes.explanation: Homer's misunderstanding of how education works is perfectly in character. Shows his flawed logic clearly.

jokes.text: I am so smart! I am so smart! S-M-R-T! I mean, S-M-A-R-T!
jokes.setup: Homer celebrating his intelligence after getting into college
jokes.punchline: Misspells 'smart' while declaring his smartness, then corrects himself
jokes.explanation: Iconic Homer moment - the irony is perfect, the self-correction makes it even funnier, and it became a cultural touchstone. Peak character comedy.

jokes.text: Hey! How long have you been back there? Three days.
jokes.setup: Homer discovers his father in the backseat
jokes.punchline: Grandpa's been there for three days without anyone noticing
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist humor with Grandpa's deadpan delivery. The impossible timeline makes it funnier.

jokes.text: There are two kinds of college students: jocks and nerds. As a jock, it is my duty to give nerds a hard time.
jokes.setup: Homer explaining college social dynamics to Marge
jokes.punchline: Homer's oversimplified worldview and self-identification as a jock
jokes.explanation: Homer's black-and-white thinking and delusional self-image as a jock is funny and in character.

jokes.text: Hey, pal! Did you get a load of the nerd? Pardon me?
jokes.setup: Homer trying to bond with real college jock
jokes.punchline: Jock doesn't understand or engage with Homer's attempt
jokes.explanation: Shows Homer's delusion about fitting in with jocks. The awkward non-response is realistic but not exceptional comedy.

jokes.text: Attention, everyone. The punch has been spiked! Don't worry. Your parents have been called and will be here to pick you up shortly!
jokes.setup: Homer at college party trying to be a party animal
jokes.punchline: His 'wild' prank is calling parents to pick up students
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - his idea of rebellion is protective parenting. Great misdirection from expected college party chaos.

jokes.text: Hi there. I'm Dean Peterson, but you can call me Bobby. I'm always up for some Hacky Sack. I used to be the bass player for the Pretenders.
jokes.setup: Homer expects crusty old dean based on movies
jokes.punchline: Dean is overly friendly hippie type, complete with fake rock credentials
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of the movie trope Homer was expecting. The try-hard coolness is relatable and funny.

jokes.text: Out with the old, in with the nucleus.
jokes.setup: Professor addressing class with old saying
jokes.punchline: Awkward modification for nuclear physics context
jokes.explanation: Weak professor humor - forced pun that doesn't quite work. The awkwardness might be intentional but doesn't land well.

jokes.text: Then kiss my curvy butt good-bye.
jokes.setup: Professor asks if students want to attend review session
jokes.punchline: Homer's crude modification of common phrase
jokes.explanation: Homer's crude humor and self-reference to his body. In character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Sir, six cinder blocks are missing. There'll be no hospital then. I'll tell the children.
jokes.setup: Homer stole blocks for his dorm room bookshelf
jokes.punchline: Six blocks somehow doom entire hospital project
jokes.explanation: Great escalation humor - minor theft causes major consequences. The deadpan delivery of tragic news makes it funnier.

jokes.text: I worked in a nuclear power plant for 10 years and I think I know how a proton accelerator works.
jokes.setup: Professor explaining physics equipment
jokes.punchline: Homer's confidence despite demonstrated incompetence
jokes.explanation: Classic Homer overconfidence. The irony works because we know his track record, building on episode's theme.

jokes.text: [Homer falls into the accelerator and screams]
jokes.setup: Homer confidently approaches scientific equipment
jokes.punchline: Immediately falls in and gets hurt
jokes.explanation: Predictable but effective physical comedy payoff to Homer's overconfidence. Simple but works.

jokes.text: Hello, Dean. You're a stupid-head!
jokes.setup: Homer making prank phone call to dean
jokes.punchline: His 'clever' insult is childishly simple
jokes.explanation: Homer's juvenile approach to pranking is in character. The childish insult contrasts with his adult status.

jokes.text: Intruder alert. Intruder alert. Stop the humanoid.
jokes.setup: Homer enters nerd dormitory
jokes.punchline: Nerds treat human visitor like sci-fi threat
jokes.explanation: Good nerd characterization - their sci-fi language for normal situations. 'Humanoid' is a nice touch.

jokes.text: We are the knights who say 'Ni!' Ni! Ni! Ni!
jokes.setup: Nerds demonstrating their knowledge
jokes.punchline: Quoting Monty Python perfectly on cue
jokes.explanation: Reference humor that perfectly fits nerd characters. Not original but characterization is spot-on.

jokes.text: We played Dungeons and Dragons for three hours. Then I was slain by an elf.
jokes.setup: Explaining their evening activities to Homer
jokes.punchline: Tragic game death delivered matter-of-factly
jokes.explanation: Good nerd humor - treating game events as real life experiences. The deadpan delivery works well.

jokes.text: Some nerds of note include popcorn magnate Orville Redenbacher, rock star David Byrne and Supreme Court Justice David Souter.
jokes.setup: Lisa defending nerds to Homer
jokes.punchline: Lists successful nerds, including unexpected Supreme Court justice
jokes.explanation: Educational humor mixing obvious nerds with unexpected ones. Lisa's earnest defense is in character.

jokes.text: Oh, not Souter!
jokes.setup: Learning Supreme Court Justice is a nerd
jokes.punchline: Homer's exaggerated horror at this revelation
jokes.explanation: Homer's dramatic reaction to minor information is funny. The specificity of caring about Souter is amusing.

jokes.text: Including all known lanthanides and actinides? Good luck.
jokes.setup: Homer wants to write periodic table on hand
jokes.punchline: Nerd's technical objection shows impossibility
jokes.explanation: Good nerd detail knowledge contrasted with Homer's simple solution. Technical accuracy makes it funnier.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. I'll put on my snow pants. Me too. Me too.
jokes.setup: Homer inviting nerds to party
jokes.punchline: Nerds dress for outdoor activity instead of party clothes
jokes.explanation: Shows nerds' social inexperience through practical clothing choice. Their excitement is endearing.

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, Gary spilled his ear medicine.
jokes.setup: College road trip to spread mayhem
jokes.punchline: Nerd's medical issue replaces typical road trip wildness
jokes.explanation: Subverts road trip expectations with medical mundanity. The specific 'ear medicine' detail is good.

jokes.text: Oh, those baby ducks were so cute. I can't believe they let us walk right up and feed them.
jokes.setup: Expected college party mayhem
jokes.punchline: Nerds enjoyed innocent duck feeding instead
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection from expected party chaos to wholesome nature activity. The innocence is charming.

jokes.text: We roll him up in a carpet and throw him off a bridge!
jokes.setup: Homer planning college prank
jokes.punchline: His prank ideas are essentially murder plots
jokes.explanation: Dark humor in Homer's extreme 'prank' ideas. The casualness with which he suggests violence is funny.

jokes.text: Meet Sir Oinkcelot, the beloved mascot of Springfield A&M.
jokes.setup: Introducing rival college's mascot
jokes.punchline: Pig mascot with ridiculous medieval name
jokes.explanation: Creative name combining pig sounds with Lancelot. The formality of presentation makes it funnier.

jokes.text: And then we roll him up in a carpet and throw him off a bridge!
jokes.setup: Nerd proposes actual prank of pig kidnapping
jokes.punchline: Homer still wants to add violent conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Homer's one-track mind about violence. The repetition and others' confusion work well.

jokes.text: Homer suggesting same carpet/bridge plan for pig kidnapping
jokes.setup: Nerds suggest kidnapping rival school's pig mascot
jokes.punchline: Homer immediately suggests his same violent 'prank' solution
jokes.explanation: Good running gag showing Homer has one solution for everything. The repetition makes his violent mindset even funnier.

jokes.text: Look, you pull its tail straight... and it curls right back up again. Curly, straight. Curly, straight.
jokes.setup: Homer playing with kidnapped pig
jokes.punchline: Treats pig tail like a toy, repetitive simple pleasure
jokes.explanation: Homer's childlike fascination with simple physical properties. The repetition shows his simple mind.

jokes.text: Curly, straight. Curly, straight. Of course he does. He's a cute little piggy.
jokes.setup: Homer playing with pig's tail
jokes.punchline: Obsessively manipulating pig's tail while calling it cute, despite obvious distress
jokes.explanation: Good Homer obliviousness moment. His fascination with simple physical properties shows childlike mindset.

jokes.text: The pig's acting kinda funny. He and Mr. Simpson split a case of malt liquor.
jokes.setup: Pig seems sick after Homer's treatment
jokes.punchline: Homer shared alcohol with the pig
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation of Homer's bad judgment. Sharing drinks with animals is perfectly Homer behavior.

jokes.text: Ah, wallet inspector. Oh, here you go. I believe that's all in order.
jokes.setup: Nerds alone in the real world after expulsion
jokes.punchline: Nerd falls for obvious 'wallet inspector' scam
jokes.explanation: Classic scam meets nerd innocence. The formality of 'all in order' makes the gullibility funnier.

jokes.text: I can't believe that worked! Hey! That's not the wallet inspector!
jokes.setup: Scammer surprised his obvious con worked
jokes.punchline: Nerd's delayed realization comes too late
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up showing both the scammer's disbelief and the victim's slow comprehension. Timing works well.

jokes.text: Mr. Simpson, we all have nosebleeds.
jokes.setup: Nerds staying at Simpson house
jokes.punchline: All simultaneously get nosebleeds, showing their physical fragility
jokes.explanation: Good nerd fragility gag. Simultaneous nosebleeds suggest they're overwhelmed by normal household environment.

jokes.text: Those crazy noises are computer signals. Some guys at M.I.T. are sending us reasons why Captain Picard is better than Captain Kirk.
jokes.setup: Homer confused by computer modem sounds
jokes.punchline: Nerds using technology for Star Trek debates
jokes.explanation: Good nerd characterization using early internet culture. The specific debate topic is perfectly nerdy.

jokes.text: Ha! They're out of their minds.
jokes.setup: Learning about Picard vs Kirk debate
jokes.punchline: Homer has strong opinion on Star Trek captains
jokes.explanation: Homer's unexpected investment in nerd culture debate. Shows his hidden geek side but not exceptional.

jokes.text: My purpose in life is to witness this moment.
jokes.setup: Watching violent cartoon where victim finally wins
jokes.punchline: Bart treats cartoon violence as life's meaning
jokes.explanation: Bart's dramatic investment in cartoon violence is perfectly in character. The existential language is funny.

jokes.text: We need the outlet for our rock tumbler. Plug it in! The TV! The TV!
jokes.setup: Nerds need electricity for their equipment
jokes.punchline: Homer prioritizes TV over nerd hobby during climactic moment
jokes.explanation: Good conflict between nerd priorities and Homer's TV addiction. The desperation in both voices works.

jokes.text: Wow! They'll never let us show that again, not in a million years!
jokes.setup: Particularly violent cartoon moment
jokes.punchline: Bart recognizes censorship implications
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about TV censorship. Bart's media awareness is funny and the line became somewhat prophetic.

jokes.text: Now, the only antidote to a zany scheme... is an even zanier scheme! Why does it have to be zany?
jokes.setup: Homer planning to fix the situation
jokes.punchline: Nerd questions the logic of escalating zaniness
jokes.explanation: Meta-humor about sitcom logic with nerd providing rational objection. Good commentary on TV tropes.

jokes.text: Should we correct for wind resistance? Hmm, possibly. What do you think? [car hits dean]
jokes.setup: Nerds overthinking rescue timing with physics
jokes.punchline: While calculating, they miss the moment and hit the dean
jokes.explanation: Perfect nerd humor - their academic approach causes the very problem they're trying to solve. Excellent irony.

jokes.text: Oh, Dean. This is what your new hip is going to look like.
jokes.setup: Dean injured in Homer's rescue scheme
jokes.punchline: Casual presentation of major surgical consequences
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about medical consequences. The casual delivery makes the severity funnier but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Number of messages received: zero.
jokes.setup: Nerds checking messages after being away for weeks
jokes.punchline: Robotic confirmation of their social isolation
jokes.explanation: Sad-funny moment highlighting nerd isolation. The mechanical delivery emphasizes their loneliness.

jokes.text: Exam? Oh! This is just like one of those bad dreams!
jokes.setup: Professor mentions final exam Homer forgot about
jokes.punchline: Homer's dramatic realization and comparison to nightmares
jokes.explanation: Relatable college anxiety moment. Homer's dramatic reaction is in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: During the exam, I'll hide under some coats... and hope that somehow everything will work out.
jokes.setup: Homer needs a plan for the exam
jokes.punchline: His 'plan' is literal hiding and magical thinking
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer logic - avoidance and wishful thinking as strategy. The specificity of hiding under coats is great.

jokes.text: Oh, I'm gonna lose my job just 'cause I'm dangerously unqualified!
jokes.setup: Homer upset about failing college
jokes.punchline: Treats dangerous incompetence as unfair technicality
jokes.explanation: Excellent Homer self-awareness mixed with complete lack of understanding. The phrase 'dangerously unqualified' became quotable.

jokes.text: Moral whuzzah?
jokes.setup: Nerd mentions moral dilemma about cheating
jokes.punchline: Homer's confusion at concept of morality
jokes.explanation: Homer's dismissal of moral complexity with made-up word. Shows his ethical simplicity amusingly.

jokes.text: Oh, let's just say I had help from a little magic box.
jokes.setup: Marge asks how Homer got good grade
jokes.punchline: Homer describes computer as 'magic box'
jokes.explanation: Homer's primitive understanding of technology. The mystical description of computers is amusing.

jokes.text: No, you didn't. You only passed your course by cheating, which you always taught us was wrong.
jokes.setup: Homer claims to have learned about hard work
jokes.punchline: Lisa points out he learned the opposite lesson
jokes.explanation: Lisa's moral clarity contrasting with Homer's self-deception. Good family dynamic and ethical point.

jokes.text: Oh, Marge! You're worse than that crusty old dean.
jokes.setup: Marge insisting Homer retake class honestly
jokes.punchline: Homer compares wife to movie stereotype he never encountered
jokes.explanation: Callback to Homer's movie-based expectations about college. The comparison to non-existent dean is funny.
